title: caucalis marilandica
intro: Caucalis marilandica, also known as Wild Carrot or American Queen Anne's Lace, is a plant species in the carrot family, native to North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Caucalis marilandica include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with herbal preparations typically using the plant's roots and leaves.
